hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, they have retained many of their original building features including their shared roofline with central gable, brick cladding, first-storey bay windows, and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched openings. The polychromatic brickwork at 6 Bellevue Avenue also remains visible. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, bay window, original windows.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: The polychromatic brickwork at 6 Bellevue Avenue also remains visible. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, they have retained many of their original building features including their shared roofline with central gable, brick cladding, first-storey bay windows, and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched openings.

facade_description: Two-storey semi-detached house (paired with 6 Bellevue Ave) in red brick with a shared central gable roofline. Features a two-storey canted projecting bay, decorative brickwork, and segmentally arched window openings. A porch is visible at front, partially screened by evergreen trees. The building is in good condition with party walls on both sides.
heritage_summary: The polychromatic brickwork at 6 Bellevue Avenue also remains visible. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, they have retained many of their original building features including their shared roofline with central gable, brick cladding, first-storey bay windows, and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched openings.

photo_observations.description: Red brick Victorian, partially visible through evergreen trees. Porch visible.
photo_observations.notes: Trees heavily obstructing view. Photo rotated.
